heading: Participation by State member banks in lotteries and related activities
label: 339 Participation by State member banks in lotteries and related activities
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as section 9A of act , and not as part of section 9 of such act which comprises this subchapter.Dec. 13, 1913

Amendments2014—Subsec. (c)(2). , inserted “, other than a savings promotion raffle,” before “whereby” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 113–251, § 3(b)(1)Subsec. (c)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 113–251, § 3(b)(2)
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Apr. 1, 1968section 6 of Pub. L. 90–203section 25a of this title
amendments: Amendments2014—Subsec. (c)(2). , inserted “, other than a savings promotion raffle,” before “whereby” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 113–251, § 3(b)(1)Subsec. (c)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 113–251, § 3(b)(2)

children.text: deal in bets used as a means or substitute for participation in a lottery;
announce, advertise, or publicize the existence of any lottery;1So in original. The word “or” probably should appear.1
announce, advertise, or publicize the existence or identity of any participant or winner, as such, in a lottery.

children.heading: Use of banking premises prohibited
children.label: b Use of banking premises prohibited

children.text: the use of any part of any of its banking offices by any person for any purpose forbidden to the bank under subsection (a), or
direct access by the public from any of its banking offices to any premises used by any person for any purpose forbidden to the bank under subsection (a).
The term “deal in” includes making, taking, buying, selling, redeeming, or collecting.

children.label: B
children.text: a game, race, or contest; or

children.label: C
children.text: any record or tabulation of the result of one or more events in which any participant has no interest except for its bearing upon the possibility that he may become a winner.

The term “lottery ticket” includes any right, privilege, or possibility (and any ticket, receipt, record, or other evidence of any such right, privilege, or possibility) of becoming a winner in a lottery.

The term “savings promotion raffle” means a contest in which the sole consideration required for a chance of winning designated prizes is obtained by the deposit of a specified amount of money in a savings account or other savings program, where each ticket or entry has an equal chance of being drawn, such contest being subject to regulations that may from time to time be promulgated by the appropriate prudential regulator (as defined in ).section 5481 of this title

children.heading: Lawful banking services connected with operation of lottery
children.label: d Lawful banking services connected with operation of lottery
children.text: Nothing contained in this section prohibits a State member bank from accepting deposits or cashing or otherwise handling checks or other negotiable instruments, or performing other lawful banking services for a State operating a lottery, or for an officer or employee of that State who is charged with the administration of the lottery.

children.heading: Regulations; enforcement
children.label: e Regulations; enforcement
children.text: The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall issue such regulations as may be necessary to the strict enforcement of this section and the prevention of evasions thereof.
